steps.title: Identify what the blank must do
steps.content: The blank describes the study's relationship to "a previous observation:" Ortega saw cold mushrooms while hiking, which raised questions — and then he conducted the study. The blank must express that the observation triggered or motivated the study.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Substitute each choice into the sentence:
  • Choice A — released in: means published or issued within — a study cannot be published inside an observation; this makes no logical sense. ✗
  • Choice B — acknowledged in: means recognized or mentioned within — this would mean the study merely noted the observation, not that the observation caused the study to be conducted. ✗
  • Choice C — excluded by: means left out or blocked by — this contradicts the passage; the observation inspired the study, not prevented it. ✗
  • Choice D — prompted by: means triggered or initiated by — Ortega's thermal-image observation raised questions that directly motivated the investigation. ✓
That matches Choice D — prompted by.
